name: Chilled Summer Squash Soup with Fresh Herbs
recipeIngredient: 4 Medium Zucchini, washed and sliced 1 inch
1 Large Yellow Crookneck Squash, washed and sliced 1 inch
Salt and freshly ground white pepper – to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine all the squash, onion, garlic, broth, and salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes or until vegetables are very tender.
2. Allow mixture to cool slightly; puree in a food processor or blender with basil, parsley, and lemon juice until smooth. Stir in buttermilk. Place in container, cover, and refrigerate for 6 hours or overnight.
3. When ready to serve, whisk until smooth and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve chilled, garnished with chopped basil and parsley.
Serves: 6
Variations:
This soup can be prepared using all zucchini, all crookneck squash, or even all pattypan squash (also known as cymling or scallop squash). It should be firm without blemishes. Fresh herbs are recommended for best flavor.
Cooking Notes:
Taste the soup after chilling to adjust seasoning, as flavors are more pronounced when hot.
